label: graph, type
constraint: The graph is directed and each node has a 'name' attribute to represent itself.

label: node, type
constraint: Each node has a 'type' attribute, in the format of EK_{TYPE}. This is important: 'type' must be a list, can include ['EK_SUPERBLOCK', 'EK_CHASSIS', 'EK_RACK', 'EK_AGG_BLOCK', 'EK_JUPITER', 'EK_PORT', 'EK_SPINEBLOCK', 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH', 'EK_CONTROL_POINT', 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'].

label: node, attribute
constraint: Each node can have other attributes depending on its type.

label: edge, type
constraint: Each directed edge also has a 'type' attribute, include RK_CONTAINS, RK_CONTROL.

label: relationship
constraint: You should check relationship based on edge, check name based on node attribute.

label: node, add
constraint: Adding new nodes need to consider all hierarchy. For example, adding a new switch requires adding it to the corresponding jupiter, aggregation block, and domain. The name to add on each layer can be inferred from new node name string.

label: edge, add
constraint: When adding new nodes, you should also add edges based on their relationship with existing nodes.

label: PORT, attribute
constraint: Each PORT node has an attribute 'physical_capacity_bps'

label: PORT, name
constraint: For example, a PORT node name is ju1.a1.m1.s2c1.p3

label: capacity
constraint: When calculating capacity of a node, you need to sum the physical_capacity_bps on the PORT of each hierarchy contains in this node.

label: node, hierarchy
constraint: Hierarchy: CHASSIS contains PACKET_SWITCH, JUPITER contains SUPERBLOCK, SUPERBLOCK contains AGG_BLOCK, AGG_BLOCK contains PACKET_SWITCH, PACKET_SWITCH contains PORT

label: graph, add
constraint: When creating a new graph, need to filter nodes and edges with attributes from the original graph.

label: graph, update
constraint: When update a graph, always create a graph copy, do not modify the input graph.

label: node, attribute
constraint: packet switch nodes also have switch location attribute 'switch_loc' in node attribute 'packet_switch_attr'.

label: node, type
constraint: To find node based on type, check the name and type list. For example, [node[0] == 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c1' and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in node[1]['type']].
